2017-05-25.abstract: The two bombers set off explosives at one of the busiest transit hubs in Indonesia’s capital as the city was preparing for Ramadan.

2017-05-26.title: Asian Kenyans Seek to Be Declared a ‘Tribe’ of Their Own
2017-05-26.abstract: Kenyans of Indian and Pakistani descent, many of whose forebears helped build the nation, want to become the country’s 44th official ethnic group.

2017-05-26.title: Leaks: A Uniquely American Way of Annoying the Authorities
2017-05-26.abstract: Is there something particularly American about leaking? Some national allergy to protecting government secrets? Yes, in fact, there is.

2017-05-26.title: Statue of Woman Removed From Bangladesh’s Supreme Court
2017-05-26.abstract: Islamic hard-liners had protested the sculpture, saying that representational art was forbidden in Islam. The sculptor said the government had capitulated to their demands.

2017-05-26.title: Gunmen in Egypt Force Coptic Christian Pilgrims From Buses and Kill 28
2017-05-26.abstract: The assault on vehicles carrying Coptic worshipers, including children, was the latest in a wave of violence targeting the Christian minority in the country.

2017-05-26.title: Philippines Strains to Clear ISIS-Inspired Militants From Southern City
2017-05-26.abstract: Indonesians, Malaysians and Singaporeans are fighting alongside Abu Sayyaf and Maute group members in Marawi, the Philippine solicitor general said.

2017-05-27.title: U.S. Quietly Lifts Limit on Number of Refugees Allowed In
2017-05-27.abstract: Despite President Trump’s efforts to curtail resettlement, the State Department’s move could result in a near doubling of refugees entering the country.

2017-05-27.title: Their Government in Chaos, Brazilians Fear the Joke Is on Them
2017-05-27.abstract: President Michel Temer has grown so unpopular that some in Brazil believe the coach of the national soccer team, or a clown named Grumpy, would be better choices.

2017-05-27.title: Mild in Mideast, Trump Is All Elbows in a Europe Eager to Jab Back
2017-05-27.abstract: The president played the statesman in the Middle East, but has switched to a familiar role, disrupter, as he joins the Group of 7 meeting, getting headlines like “Boor in Chief.”

2017-05-27.title: Zbigniew Brzezinski, National Security Adviser to Jimmy Carter, Dies at 89
2017-05-27.abstract: Mr. Brzezinski, who guided Mr. Carter during the Iran hostage crisis and the Soviet invasion of Afghanistan, had decades of influence in global affairs.

2017-05-27.title: Taliban Car Bomb Targeting Elite Afghan Force Kills at Least 12
2017-05-27.abstract: Some officials put the death toll as high as 18 after the explosion in a crowded bazaar.

2017-05-28.title: Brazil’s Crisis: How Fighting Corruption Could Imperil Political Stability
2017-05-28.abstract: President Temer may be ousted just like his predecessor, Dilma Rousseff, prompting fears the country’s political institutions may struggle to regain credibility and maintain stability.

2017-05-28.title: Timeline Shows Manchester Bomber’s Family’s Deep Ties to Libya
2017-05-28.abstract: Salman Abedi, who killed 22 people in a terrorist attack, visited North Africa with his brother and father, who worked to overthrow Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi.

2017-05-28.title: Canada’s Conservatives Choose Andrew Scheer as Their New Leader
2017-05-28.abstract: Mr. Scheer, a 38-year-old social conservative, has said that the party needs “to have something positive to say.”

2017-05-28.title: 1917: Pro-German “Gloaters” Listed by Federal Agents
2017-05-28.abstract: A short article outlining the federal government’s desire to control private and public discourse during World War One.

2017-05-28.title: Will Russia Stick to Promise to Cut Output With OPEC? Skepticism Abounds
2017-05-28.abstract: The Kremlin has a long history of trying to jawbone up global oil prices while doing as little as possible, or nothing, to curb output.

2017-05-29.title: Andrew Scheer, Canada’s Conservative Leader, Has Yet to Define Himself
2017-05-29.abstract: Andrew Scheer won leadership of the Conservative Party with a platform light on promises. He could be prime minister if he can defeat Justin Trudeau’s Liberals in 2019.

2017-05-29.title: Foot Soldiers in a Shadowy Battle Between Russia and the West
2017-05-29.abstract: Freelance agitators promote Russia’s agenda abroad, getting their backing from tycoons and others, not the state itself.

2017-05-29.title: Christian School Teaches Scions of the Elite in Atheist North Korea
2017-05-29.abstract: In a country that bans religion, the school educates a crop of handpicked students — and perhaps gives the government some leverage with Washington.

2017-05-29.title: Philippine Forces Struggle to Retake City Held by Islamist Militants
2017-05-29.abstract: An official called on civilians to help the military clear Marawi, a city in the south, of insurgents from ISIS-linked groups who are proving difficult to dislodge.

2017-05-29.title: China Charges Activist From Taiwan With ‘Subverting State Power’
2017-05-29.abstract: The mainland authorities have never leveled that charge against someone from Taiwan before, and his formal arrest adds a new strain to tensions between Beijing and Taipei.

2017-05-30.title: Egypt’s President Enacts Law Placing Severe Restrictions on Aid Groups
2017-05-30.abstract: The move by President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi stoked fears that his government intends to accelerate its harsh crackdown on human rights activists.

2017-05-30.title: With Italy No Longer in U.S. Focus, Russia Swoops to Fill the Void
2017-05-30.abstract: Russia has made a concerted effort to build ties with Italy, which many officials believe is creating a slow but certain tilt toward Moscow.

2017-05-30.title: Theresa May Doesn’t Crack and Jeremy Corbyn Keeps His Cool in U.K. Debate
2017-05-30.abstract: The prime minister did not sustain further political damage, but she did not banish doubts about her leadership that were raised by a policy reversal last week.

2017-05-30.title: Why Noriega’s Ouster Still Reverberates
2017-05-30.abstract: Removing Manuel Noriega, the dictator of Panama, from power in 1989 not only entailed what was then the largest American military action since Vietnam, but also set the stage for future actions by the United States.

2017-05-30.title: Manuel Noriega, Dictator Ousted by U.S. in Panama, Dies at 83
2017-05-30.abstract: Mr. Noriega, known for drug ties and double-dealing with Washington, was unseated in 1989 in what was then the largest American military action since Vietnam.

2017-05-31.title: Cholera, Famine and Girls Sold Into Marriage for Food: Yemen’s Dire Picture
2017-05-31.abstract: United Nations officials reported surging malnutrition and preventable disease in the war-torn nation, with nearly seven million a step away from famine.

2017-05-31.title: Blind Spots in Trump’s Trade Tirade Against Germany
2017-05-31.abstract: President Trump’s campaign against the U.S. trade deficit with Germany overlooks some crucial benefits and overstates Berlin’s ability to do much about it.

2017-05-31.title: Australia Plans to Revoke Child Sex Offenders’ Passports to Combat Sex Tourism
2017-05-31.abstract: Pedophiles on Australia’s sex offender registry would have their passports taken away to keep them from traveling overseas to engage in the child sex trade.

2017-05-31.title: Deadly Bombing in Kabul Is One of the Afghan War’s Worst Strikes
2017-05-31.abstract: The blast near the Afghan presidential palace and the German Embassy, which killed over 80 and wounded hundreds, spoke volumes to the frustrations of stabilizing the country.

2017-05-31.title: China Woos South Korea’s New Leader, but the U.S. Left Behind a Spoiler
2017-05-31.abstract: A missile defense system that was set up just before Moon Jae-in took office is complicating efforts by the Chinese president, Xi Jinping, to fortify his influence in the region.
